reason: Multiple edges align for Arizona. Pfaadt is elite and scorching hot (recent ERA 0.89, xERA 2.41) with a strong matchup vs STL (2.60 ERA in 3 career starts vs them). STL offense is dangerously cold (wRC+ 46 in L12, worst in this slate) and striking out at 24.2% vs RHP. McGreevy's HH% 47% and Barrel% 15% are alarming contact-quality flags, and his recent ERA (3.71) is nearly identical to xERA (3.56) — no luck masking here. ARI bullpen is solid (xERA 3.25). STL averaging only 2.2 RS in home starts this stretch, going 2-3 in those. ARI team trends show 8-2 L10 with 6.7 RS in away starts. Getting ARI at +102 is mispriced given the pitching and offensive mismatch — the market is treating this closer than it should be.

Pure Pfaadt dominance bet isolated from game outcome or ARI bullpen. STL wRC+ 46 vs RHP in L12 is the coldest offense on the slate. Pfaadt's recent ERA is 0.89 over his last 3 starts, xERA is 2.41 — elite. He has a 2.60 ERA in 3 career starts vs STL. STL averages only 2.2 RS per game in home starts under their current stretch. Getting +114 on STL Under 3.5 is excellent value — this line should be -130 or worse given Pfaadt's form and STL's offensive futility.

Pfaadt averages 4.7 K/gs vs STL historically. STL's K% vs RHP is 24.2%, which is above average — a genuine strikeout-prone lineup. His recent average is only 4.0 K/start, but his last three opponents (SDP, SFG, LAD) are not particularly high-K teams, and the LAD outing was only 1 IP. Adjusted for STL's 24.2% K rate, expectation rises toward 5+. Getting Over 3.5 at +106 is strong value — this line is too low given his baseline vs a strikeout-prone STL lineup, the historical matchup K rate (4.7/gs), and his elite recent form.

Bradley is an elite K prop candidate today. His recent avg is 9.3 K/start (10K, 11K, 7K in last 3). The flag notes recent opponents were high-K (CLE 28%, LAA 25%, CHC 25%) — but critically, TODAY's opponent IS Cleveland (28% K%), the same high-K team he already torched for 10 Ks in 7 IP in his most recent start. He has 1 career start vs CLE: 1.29 ERA, 7.0 IP, 10 Ks. His K% is 28.6%, CLE's K% vs RHP is 27.5% — a premium two-sided strikeout matchup. His avg IP is 6.3 and recent pitch counts are 99-101-97, so he'll work deep. The inflation warning is actually a GREEN LIGHT here — the high-K opponents were the same CLE and similar lineups, not a fluke. Getting Over 6.5 at +118 is outstanding value against a 9.3 K/start average with this specific matchup history.

Troy Melton is elite and blazing hot: xERA 2.24, recent ERA 0.99, averaging 6.0 K/start over last 3 with pitch counts under 90 (84/88/84), indicating he'll go deep cleanly. His home ERA is 1.05 in 3 starts. The KCR bullpen (xERA 5.37) is a disqualifier for full-game ML bets on DET, but F5 isolates Melton entirely. On the other side, Dobnak is wild (BB% 12.9%) but has kept runs off the board (recent ERA 2.20). DET offense is only wRC+ 95 vs RHP and KCR's offense, while above-avg (wRC+ 126), faces a dominant Melton. The F5 is largely about Melton suppressing KCR for 5 innings — recent opponents were high-K (NYY 26%, ATH 27%, LAA 25%), and KCR's K% vs RHP is 20.9%, slightly below those levels, meaning we might calibrate KCR Ks down modestly. But the run-suppression case is solid — Melton hasn't allowed more than 1 ER in any of his last 3 starts. F5 Under 4.5 at -114 captures Melton's dominance without exposing to KCR's bullpen.

Strong over case with multiple signals aligning. Bieber is in acute struggle: recent ERA 9.51, xERA 4.71, ERA 5.52 (ERA > xERA means he's genuinely bad, not just unlucky). HH% 48%, BB% 12.3% — batters squaring him up AND taking free bases. His 3-start ERA is skewed by a blowup but even the 'better' starts show 5.1IP/2ER and 3.2IP/4ER — not good. TBR offense is avg (wRC+ 106) vs RHP, but facing a struggling Bieber with a 4.9 IP/gs average means this goes to TOR bullpen (xERA 3.70, ERA 5.44) early. Seymour has flipped his season around (recent ERA 3.24 vs season ERA 8.49) but his HH% 46% and Barrel% 14% remain dangerous — TOR offense, even at wRC+ 46 vs LHP (poor), can still make contact (HH% 43.3%). Both bullpens are leaky (TBR xERA 3.52, TOR xERA 3.70; TOR ERA 5.44 is alarming). Both teams' bullpens are flagged as stressed/elevated. The park is Rogers Centre (domed/open air — neutral). Total at 8.5 is achievable: Bieber's implosion risk alone pushes expected runs over 5+, and Seymour's high contact rates suggest TOR bullpen exposure. Over signals: both xERAs ≥ 4.70, Bieber recent ERA >> xERA (struggling acutely), both bullpens average or worse, Bieber's avg IP only 4.9 means heavy bullpen exposure from TOR side.
